meaningEn: You damn fool! — a feisty Edo insult
meaningJa: ばかやろう（江戸っ子の啖呵）
exampleJa: べらぼうめ、なめんじゃねえ！
exampleEn: You damn fool — don't mess with me!
socialConsequenceEn: Yell this today and you instantly become a samurai-drama grandpa. Glorious, but nobody talks like this anymore.

tag.sourceNote: Edo-period rough speech; survives in jidaigeki

meaningEn: What're you on about?! — a blustering retort
meaningJa: 何を言ってやがる（の江戸訛り）
exampleJa: てやんでい、こちとら江戸っ子でい！
exampleEn: What're you on about?! I'm a true Edo native!
socialConsequenceEn: Peak Edo bravado. Say it and you sound like you stepped out of a 1960s samurai film.

tag.sourceNote: 「何を言ってるんだ」の江戸訛り

meaningEn: Get lost! (lit. 'come back the day before yesterday')
meaningJa: 二度と来るな（追い返す決まり文句）
exampleJa: そんな話、おととい来やがれ！
exampleEn: A deal like that? Get outta here!
socialConsequenceEn: A theatrical 'buzz off'. Hilarious and harmless now — pure old-Tokyo flavor.

tag.sourceNote: 来られない日を指す江戸の追い返し表現

meaningJa: 洗練されていない、無粋
exampleJa: それを聞くのは野暮ってもんだ。
exampleRomaji: sore wo kiku no wa yabo tte mon da.
socialConsequenceEn: Still perfectly usable today — an Edo word that never went out of style.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の美意識(粋の対義)。現代も使う

meaningEn: Chic; stylish in an understated, cool way
meaningJa: 洗練されて格好いい、あか抜けた様子
exampleJa: あの人の着こなしは粋だね。
exampleRomaji: ano hito no kikonashi wa iki da ne.
exampleEn: The way they dress is so chic.
socialConsequenceEn: A compliment that still lands. Calling someone 粋 makes you sound cultured.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の美意識の中核。現代も使う

meaningJa: 間抜け、とんちんかんな人をののしる語
exampleJa: このすっとこどっこいめ！
socialConsequenceEn: So old-fashioned it loops back to funny. Drop it as a joke and people will grin.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の悪態。落語等で残る

socialConsequenceEn: A vintage compliment. Sounds like you stepped out of a festival.

tag.sourceNote: 江戸の美意識(粋・勇み肌)

meaningJa: 生粋で歯切れがよい様子
exampleJa: ちゃきちゃきの江戸っ子だ。
socialConsequenceEn: Still used, a little old-timey. Charming.

tag.sourceNote: 「ちゃきちゃきの江戸っ子」慣用句

meaningEn: More than good enough; can't complain
meaningJa: 大いに有り難い・十分なこと
自分(たち)を指す伝法な言い方
exampleJa: こちとら江戸っ子でい！
socialConsequenceEn: Tough-guy Edo speech. Pure jidaigeki energy.

exampleJa: 給料日前ですっからかん。
socialConsequenceEn: Edo-flavored but still used. Safe and vivid.

exampleJa: べらんめえ口調で啖呵を切る。
exampleRomaji: beranmee chou de tanka wo kiru.
exampleEn: He rattles off in beranmee style.
socialConsequenceEn: The classic Edo tough-talk. Theatrical now.
